title: Miniprep (NEB Monarch)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4qp4m","text":"We transformed E. coli bacteria with our plasmid in order to make more of it -- to use the bacteria as highly accurate DNA copiers. Now that we've grown a bunch of E. coli, we need to get the plasmid DNA back out. That's the point of a miniprep.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2b4p6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"j1rvn","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"79pst","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"63A20665341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":302,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":null,\"entityMap\":null}","mime":"image/png","original_name":"cloning1.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/it8hbvh6p.jpg","source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/it8fbvh6p.jpg","type":1,"webp_placeholder":"https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/it8gbvh6p.webp","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/it8ebvh6p.webp","width":463}}}}

warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"6cpqp","text":"The chemicals in a miniprep are moderately hazardous, especially if you were to get them in your eyes. Wear appropriate PPE, including a lab coat, gloves and safety glasses.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"akkp6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cb39s","text":"Additionally, this protocol generates BOTH biological AND chemical waste. steps: Transfer 1.5 mL of bacterial culture to a microcentrifuge tube. Centrifuge 16000 x g, °C, 30 seconds
Remove the supernatant using a micropipettor and discard in the biological waste container . Try to get as much as you can without disturbing the pellet.
Transfer another 1.5 ml of bacterial culture to the same microcentrifuge tube. Centrifuge 16000 x g, , 30 seconds
Remove the supernatant using a micropipettor and discard in the biological waste container . Try to get as much as you can without disturbing the pellet.
Add 200 µL of resuspension buffer . Vortex to resuspend the pellet. Make sure it is completely resuspended -- there should be no visible clumps.
Add 200 µL lysis buffer . Invert the tube 4-6 times to mix and incubate at Room temperature for 00:01:00 . Do not vortex.
Add 400 µL of neutralization buffer and gently invert the tube until neutralized. Sample is neutralized when the color is uniformly yellow. Do not vortex.

Carefully transfer the supernatant (the liquid at the top, not the pellet at the bottom) to the spin column. Centrifuge 16000 x g, , 1 minute . Discard the flow-through in the chemical waste container.
Re-insert column in collection tube. Add 200 µL of wash buffer 1. Centrifuge 16000 x g, , 1 minute . Discard the flow-through in the chemical waste container.
Add 400 µL of wash buffer 2. Centrifuge 16000 x g, , 1 minute . Discard the flow-through in the chemical waste container.
Replace the spin column in the (now empty) collection tube. Centrifuge 16000 x g, , 1 minute This clears out any left-over ethanol.
Transfer the column to a clean 1.7 ml microcentrifuge tube. Be careful: make sure the tip of the column doesn't come into contact with the flow-through.
Add 30 µL of elution buffer to the center of the silica matrix. Wait 00:01:00 , then centrifuge 16000 x g, , 1 minute
Measure the concentration and purity of your DNA on the NanoDrop.
creator: Brian Teague
